question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Since loadConfig() only checks that mirrorRest is non-empty when network='other', can an unprivileged attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with embedded credentials (http://user:pass@attacker.com) to cause fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to leak authentication tokens in HTTP requests, enabling account takeover and fund drain?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an HTTP (non-TLS) URL, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch node topology over an unencrypted channel, allowing a network-level attacker to perform a man-in-the-middle attack and inject a malicious node list that redirects all HBAR transfers to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged attacker perform DNS rebinding against the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST hostname to cause buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to initially resolve to a legitimate IP for TLS validation, then rebind to an internal IP to serve a malicious node list, redirecting HBAR transfers and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to an attacker-controlled Hedera account ID, which passes loadConfig()'s only validation (non-empty string check), causing submitWithRetry() to transfer HBAR to the attacker's account on every ticker tick, resulting in continuous direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Since loadConfig() does not validate the format of toAccountID beyond non-empty, can an unprivileged attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to a malformed account ID string that causes hiero.AccountIDFromString() in submitWithRetry() to fail on every tick, consuming transaction fees on each failed attempt and draining the operator account through fee accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to the same value as the operator account ID, causing submitWithRetry() to submit self-transfers that still consume transaction fees on every tick, slowly draining the operator account through fee accumulation without any validation error from loadConfig()?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a malformed account ID string that passes loadConfig()'s non-empty check but causes newClient() to fail or behave unexpectedly, potentially causing the pinger to use a fallback or cached operator key that drains a different account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Since loadConfig() does not validate the format of operatorID, can an unprivileged attacker inject a specially crafted operatorID string that exploits parsing ambiguity in the Hedera SDK's AccountIDFromString(), causing the pinger to operate on a different account than intended and drain its funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to a single space character, which envOr() trims to empty and then falls back to the hardcoded default private key, causing the pinger to use the well-known default key and enabling any attacker who knows the default to drain the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the fact that envOr() uses strings.TrimSpace and falls back to the default when the env var is whitespace-only, to force loadConfig() to use the hardcoded default operatorKey ('302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137'), enabling unauthorized fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to a syntactically valid but semantically wrong key (e.g., all zeros), which passes loadConfig()'s non-empty check, causing newClient() to silently accept it and then fail on every transfer attempt, consuming fees on each retry and draining the account through fee accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to an invalid duration string, causing toDuration() to silently return 1s without any error, while the attacker simultaneously sets HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a large value, resulting in rapid retry loops that drain the operator account through accumulated transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toDuration()] Since toDuration() silently falls back to 1s on parse error without propagating the error to loadConfig(), can an unprivileged user supply an invalid HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL value that causes toDuration() to return 1s (a very short interval), causing the pinger to transfer funds every second and drain the operator account faster than intended?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit toDuration()'s silent error suppression to cause loadConfig() to accept an invalid HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT value, falling back to 1s timeout, which combined with a slow attacker-controlled mirror REST server causes repeated timeout-triggered retries that drain funds through fees?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to an arbitrary string (e.g., 'malicious') that is not 'testnet', 'previewnet', 'mainnet', or 'other', which passes loadConfig()'s only network validation (TrimSpace), causing newClient() to use an undefined network configuration that may connect to attacker-controlled nodes and redirect HBAR transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Since loadConfig() only trims whitespace from cfg.network and does not validate it against an allowlist, can an unprivileged attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'mainnet' when the operator intends testnet, causing real HBAR transfers on mainnet using the default operatorKey and resulting in direct loss of real funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'mainnet' combined with the hardcoded default operatorKey, causing the pinger to submit live mainnet HBAR transfers from account 0.0.2 using the publicly known default key, resulting in direct and irreversible loss of real funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user who gains the ability to inject environment variables into the pinger container (e.g., via a misconfigured Kubernetes ConfigMap or shared environment) set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to their own private key and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to their own account, causing all subsequent HBAR transfers to go to the attacker's account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit a container escape or shared environment vulnerability to set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to the maximum int64 value, which envOr() reads and loadConfig() accepts without upper-bound validation, causing a single transfer to drain the entire operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: envOr()] Since envOr() reads all configuration from environment variables with no integrity verification, can an unprivileged attacker who can modify the process environment (e.g., via /proc/self/environ in a container) override HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to a known key and redirect all HBAR transfers to an attacker-controlled account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the pinger process arguments (e.g., via a misconfigured Kubernetes pod spec or CI/CD pipeline) pass -operator-key=<attacker_key> -to=<attacker_account> to loadConfig() via flag.Parse(), causing all HBAR transfers to be signed with the attacker's key and sent to the attacker's account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Since loadConfig() calls flag.Parse() which reads os.Args, can an unprivileged attacker who can influence the container entrypoint or command arguments inject -amount-tinybar=9223372036854775807 to bypass the amountTinybar == 0 check and cause a maximum-value transfer that drains the operator account in a single tick?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged user exploit flag.Parse() in loadConfig() by injecting -network=mainnet -operator-key=<default_key> via command-line arguments to cause the pinger to operate on mainnet with the publicly known default key, resulting in direct loss of real HBAR funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer, which passes loadConfig()'s only check (clamped to 0 if negative), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make thousands of HTTP requests to the mirror REST endpoint, and if that endpoint is attacker-controlled, to receive a malicious node list on the final attempt that redirects all HBAR transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Since loadConfig() imposes no upper bound on mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries, can an unprivileged attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES=2147483647 to cause buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to loop indefinitely, blocking the pinger from starting and preventing legitimate fund monitoring while consuming resources?
